concerns.text: Task T005 hardcodes dataset 'MixSub-LLaMA-3.2' which lacks human ratings. Plan.md Phase 0 explicitly requires acquiring a dataset with ratings or defining an annotation protocol. No task exists to implement Plan.md Step 0.1 (verify dataset) or Step 0.2 (annotation protocol), leaving FR-009 (human ratings) and SC-006 (inter-rater reliability) unimplemented.

Tasks T029-T033 implement 'Revision Concerns' (dual-rating inputs, noise analysis, terminology shifts) that have no corresponding Functional Requirement or Success Criterion in spec.md. Spec.md Assumptions explicitly state 'no multi-dimensional subscale analysis is assumed' and FR-009 mandates a single scalar score. These tasks represent scope creep without spec backing.

Task T013 detects skewness (FR-008) but no task explicitly describes generating the required report/log entry to 'flag the data in the report with suggested transformations' as mandated by FR-008 and Edge Cases. The output of T013 is not connected to a reporting task.

Tasks T029-T033 reference 'Daniel Kahneman-simulated review' and 'cognitive distinctions' which are not present in the spec.md or plan.md. These tasks appear to be based on external or hallucinated requirements rather than the approved specification.
